{{Quote|Some of Meeko's thugs have decent prices on their heads, too.|Rozatta informs Jango Fett of Teep's bounty|<ref name="BH" />}}
'''Burbakker Teep''' was a [[Gran/Legends|Gran]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who lived during the last [[Standard decade/Legends|decades]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. An [[Arms dealer/Legends|arms dealer]] and [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] by [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], he was implicated in attacks that destroyed or severely damaged several [[Republic Security Force/Legends|Republic Security Force]] facilities. For these crimes, a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] was placed on his head by the RSF, offering 1,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] for his capture alive and 500 credits dead.

By the [[Time/Legends|time]] the bounty was placed, Teep had fallen in with the smugglers of [[Muttani]] crime boss [[Meeko Ghintee]]'s syndicate. The [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|Bounty hunter]] [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] ran into Teep on [[Outland Transit Station|Outland Station]], where he was attempting to help Ghintee escape from the station's pit fighting arena. Having been informed of Teep's bounty by the [[Toydarian/Legends|Toydarian]] owner of the station, [[Rozatta]], Jango captured him alive for 1,000 credits.<ref name="BH" />

==Behind the scenes==
Burbakker Teep first appeared in the [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter]]'', released by [[LucasArts]] in [[2002]]. He is the first of five possible secondary bounties that players can secure in the outer arena section of the first chapter of the game. As the game's very first secondary bounty, he is unarmed and used as the tutorial to show players how to capture secondary bounties within the game. Players have the option of either killing him or taking him alive with this article assuming he was captured alive as it offered a higher reward. Teep was also mentioned in the [[Prima]] ''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter: Prima's Official Strategy Guide]],'' which was released alongside the game.
